Package nonprofitbookkeeping.service

Class InventoryService

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.service.InventoryService
public class InventoryService extends Object
InventoryService manages inventory items for the nonprofit bookkeeping system. It maintains an in-memory map of inventory items, keyed by their unique ID, and provides methods to list, add, update, delete, and manage these items, including a placeholder for applying depreciation.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    InventoryService()
    Constructs an InventoryService and initializes an empty inventory map.

  • Constructor Details

    • InventoryService

      public InventoryService()
      Constructs an InventoryService and initializes an empty inventory map. Optionally, sample data can be pre-populated here during development or testing.

  • Method Details

    • listItems

      public List<InventoryItem> listItems()
      Retrieves a list of all inventory items currently managed by this service.
      Returns:
      A new ArrayList containing all InventoryItem objects. This is a copy, so modifications to the returned list will not affect internal storage. Returns an empty list if no items are present.

    • addItem

      public void addItem(InventoryItem item)
      Adds a new inventory item to the service's collection. If the provided item is null or its ID (retrieved by InventoryItem.getId()) is null, the item is not added. If an item with the same ID already exists, it will be overwritten.
      Parameters:
      item - The InventoryItem to add.

    • updateItem

      public void updateItem(InventoryItem item)
      Updates an existing inventory item in the collection. The item is identified by its ID. If the provided item is null, its ID is null, or if no item with that ID exists in the inventory, no action is taken.
      Parameters:
      item - The InventoryItem containing the updated information. The item's ID is used to find the existing item to update.

    • deleteItem

      public void deleteItem(String id)
      Deletes an inventory item from the collection based on its ID. If the provided id is null, or if no item with that ID exists, no action is taken.
      Parameters:
      id - The unique identifier (ID) of the inventory item to delete.

    • applyYearlyDepreciation

      public void applyYearlyDepreciation()
      Applies yearly depreciation to all applicable inventory items.

      Note: This is currently a placeholder method (stub) and needs to be implemented. The implementation should iterate through items, check their depreciation method and rate, calculate depreciation, and update their accumulated depreciation and net book value.

    • clearInventory

      public void clearInventory()
      Clears all items from the inventory managed by this service instance. After this operation, the inventory will be empty.

    • saveItems

      public void saveItems(File companyDirectory) throws IOException
      Saves all inventory items to the database.
      Parameters:
      companyDirectory - unused but preserved for backwards compatibility
      Throws:
      IOException - if the database write fails

    • loadItems

      public void loadItems(File companyDirectory) throws IOException
      Loads inventory items from the database.
      Parameters:
      companyDirectory - unused but preserved for backwards compatibility
      Throws:
      IOException - if the database read fails

    • getInventoryItems

      public static List<String[]> getInventoryItems()
      Retrieves a list of inventory items, formatted as arrays of strings. Note: This is a stub implementation and currently returns null. The intended structure of the string arrays (e.g., which fields are included and in what order) is not defined by the stub.
      Returns:
      A list of string arrays, where each array represents an inventory item's data, or null if the implementation is not complete.
